channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-18
item.description:
Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. In **scienceverse/metacheck**, updates were made to module names and the DESCRIPTION file, while **giladfeldman/docpluck** added a new feature to the render module and updated documentation. Meanwhile, **Davidvandijcke/coarse** made notable strides in internationalization, adding manual language switching and localization to the website UI. These efforts towards i18n are also reflected in **giladfeldman/escicheck**, which performed several sync operations with various effectcheck versions. Additionally, **JamieCummins/regcheck** prepared for production with numerous fixes and additions, including updates to the frontend and handling mechanisms. Other projects, such as **shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve** and **giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward**, also saw updates, including revisions to validation notes and the addition of a Chrome Webstore link. These connections and advancements demonstrate the community's dedication to collaborative progress and improvement.

108 commits across 7 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

regcheck33 commits
docpluck30 commits
metacheck26 commits
coarse16 commits
escicheck1 commits

item.title: metacheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `new_modules` branch saw a revert of the fix for app instruction #314, as stated in the commit message "Revert 'fix app instruction #314'", which was immediately preceded by a commit that fixed app instruction #314. The `main` branch had its module names updated in vignettes, according to the commit "update module names in vignettes". Additionally on the `main` branch, the DESCRIPTION was updated to fix #313, as per the commit "Update DESCRIPTION fix #313". The `new_modules` branch also had a fix for #313, as indicated by the commit "fix #313". Work was done on both the `main` and `new_modules` branches.

2 commit(s) today:

  • 2b8a367 [new_modules] Revert "fix app instruction #314" — Lakens
  • 1d3719b [new_modules] fix app instruction #314 — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 4 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was the focus of recent work, where a feature was added to the render module to "never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings" as of version 2.4.92. On the `main` branch, documentation was updated to reflect that version 2.4.91 has been shipped, including a handoff and cycle journal. Additionally on the `main` branch, a chore commit was made to onboard efendic, maier, and xiao into the rotating pool, marking the rotation as now active. The `main` branch also received a commit from Gilad Feldman, but the details of this commit are not specified due to the message being cut off.

4 commit(s) today:

  • cf420ca [main] feat(render): never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings (v2.4.92) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: coarse: 15 commits today
item.description:
Davidvandijcke/coarse

Work was done on the 'dev', 'feat/231-site-i18n', and 'feat/229-i18n-labels' and 'feat/226-i18n-deenglish' branches. The 'dev' branch saw the addition of a manual language switcher and localization of the website UI, as well as the localization of review headers and completion email, with the commit message stating "feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H) (#231) (#232)" and "feat(i18n): localized review headers + completion email (PR-E) (#229) (#230)". The 'dev' branch also had the analysis "de-English-ified" with the commit message "feat(i18n): de-English-ify the analysis (PR-C) (#226) (#228)", and auto-detection, a web language picker, and RTL render were added with the commit message "feat(i18n): auto-detection + web language picker + RTL render (PR-B2) (#223) (#225)". Additionally, GLM 5.2 was added to the model picker with the commit message "feat(web): add GLM 5.2 to the model picker (#227)". The 'feat/231-site-i18n', 'feat/229-i18n-labels', and 'feat/226-i18n-deenglish' branches had similar changes as their corresponding 'dev' branch commits.

15 commit(s) today:

  • 8faa90a [dev] feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H) (#231) (#232) — David Van Dijcke
  • 395a58c [feat/231-site-i18n] feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H) (#231) — Davidvandijcke
  • eae44c1 [dev] feat(i18n): localized review headers + completion email (PR-E) (#229) (#230) — David Van Dijcke
  • db35eb5 [feat/229-i18n-labels] feat(i18n): localized review headers + completion email (PR-E) (#229) — Davidvandijcke
  • ef7b339 [dev] feat(i18n): de-English-ify the analysis (PR-C) (#226) (#228) — David Van Dijcke
  • 3117681 [dev] feat(i18n): auto-detection + web language picker + RTL render (PR-B2) (#223) (#225) — David Van Dijcke
  • 8510764 [feat/226-i18n-deenglish] feat(i18n): de-English-ify the analysis (PR-C) (#226) — Davidvandijcke
  • a12f9dc [dev] feat(web): add GLM 5.2 to the model picker (#227) — David Van Dijcke

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 16 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The staging branch was the focus of the recent work, with multiple commits aimed at preparing the repository for production. The commits included fixes such as "fix oss handling bug" and "fix scroll and gpt-oss bugs", as well as additions like "add html, osf support" and "add claude functionality". The repository also saw changes to its handling mechanisms, including "harden osf handling", "harden osf handling again", and "harden qwen handling", to strengthen its overall robustness. Additionally, the commits included updates to the frontend, such as "frontend updates - light mode!" and "Update privacy.html", to enhance the user experience. The repository was also modified to "replace deprecated llama" and "change llama model to best available", indicating efforts to keep the technology up-to-date.

16 commit(s) today:

  • 4b7a527 [staging] patch oss SDK handler — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

Work was done on the 'main' branch, where several sync operations were performed, including syncing from escicheckapp@f52d7ce for effectcheck v0.6.3, escicheckapp@e060e98 for effectcheck v0.6.2, escicheckapp@5093197 for effectcheck v0.6.1, and escicheckapp@1ebe05f for effectcheck v0.5.18. A temporary branch 'sync/20260525-150441-b801333' was also used for a sync operation from escicheckapp@b801333 for effectcheck v0.6.2. The commit messages specifically mention syncing from various escicheckapp versions, as stated in the messages, such as "sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3)". The sync operations were performed by both Gilad Feldman and github-actions[bot].

1 commit(s) today:

  • 4be3331 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3) (#17) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: 1 commit today
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was updated with a new commit that added a Chrome Webstore link for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. This specific change involved adding a link, with no other features or fixes mentioned in the provided commit messages. The commit message directly mentions the addition of the Chrome Webstore link, indicating this was the sole purpose of the commit. There are no other changes or additions to report from the given commits.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 71c2a30 [main] Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF — giovannacdelsordo
  • aa35045 [main] Add detectable statements and data repositories list — giovannacdelsordo

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
